Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, marked proteinuria, high urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubin, glucose, ketones). These suggest impaired renal function, likely nephrotic-range proteinuria, and possible underlying kidney disease. Low HbA1c and normal glucose argue against diabetes. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended.
